description: Support the growth or development of a person, living thing, ability, or relationship through sustained care. Nourishment and teaching may be part of it, with the developing recipient as its concern. 'theama' (care) has a wider range, while 'lumera' (nourish) is material sustenance.
articulatory_notes: Set the tongue tip against the backs of the upper teeth for dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and open into rounded high-back 'u'. Close the lips for stressed 'm' and release into middle-height front 'e'. Touch the tongue tip to the alveolar ridge for 'l' and finish on rounded middle-height back 'o'.
sound_symbolism: Dental and bilabial nasals keep the first two syllables resonant before the lateral opens into final 'o'. The word settles rather than snaps shut, much like care that stays through development.
usage_notes: 'numelo' takes the developing person, living thing, ability, or relationship as its object. An instrumental phrase can name a means of nurture. 'temoe' (tend) attends through recurring practical acts; 'numelo' follows the longer development those acts support.

semantic_domains.community: nurture within family, friendship, or another continuing relation
semantic_domains.nature: care that supports the development of a living thing
